source: Gibson factory drawings and measured specifications. Body outline from DXF (gibson_explorer_body.dxf, 24-point coarse polygon). Model stub from guitars/explorer.py (Wave 14). Hardware and construction references from Gibson '58 Explorer reissue specs and Guitar Plans archive.

original_1958.description: 1958 Gibson Explorer — original korina (African limba) body, PAF humbuckers, gold hardware. Only ~19 original examples were shipped in 1958–59, making it one of the rarest production guitars ever made.

neck.note: The '58 Explorer has a long set-neck tenon extending to the 22nd fret area. The neck joins at or near the end of the fretboard. Rosewood fretboard with dot inlays.

headstock.note: Distinctive split-V headstock design. All 6 tuners on one side in a split arrangement. The headstock shape echoes the angular body design.

reissue_76.description: 1976 Gibson Explorer reissue — mahogany body, updated headstock shape, standard dot inlays. The Explorer that popularized the shape for rock and metal guitarists.

headstock.note: Revised pointed headstock introduced with the 1976 reissue.

outline_dxf_dimensions.note: DXF extents are larger than true body due to coordinate space. The actual Explorer body is approximately 475mm wide (horn tip to horn tip) x 460mm long (base to neck joint). DXF is AC1024 format — should be converted to R12 (AC1009) per project conventions.

body.note: The Explorer body is a dramatically angular design with two pointed horns extending from a central mass. The upper horn (treble side) extends further than the lower horn (bass side), creating the distinctive asymmetric silhouette. Flat top and flat back — no carved top. Original '58 was korina (African limba wood); reissues typically use mahogany.

neck.note: Neck humbucker routed from rear. PAF on '58 original, various humbuckers on reissues.

bridge.note: Bridge humbucker. Angled slightly for treble response on Gibson models.
ABR-1 Tune-O-Matic bridge. Stud holes drilled through body. Bridge position calculated: body_length (460) - scale_length (628.65) + neck_length_to_body_join (~476.25) = 307.6mm from top of body.

tailpiece.note: Lightweight aluminum stoptail bar, same as Les Paul. Positioned 25.4mm behind bridge centerline.

neck_pocket.note: Explorer neck joint is at the very top of the body. Long tenon set neck — the tenon extends deep into the body for strength. The neck angle is approximately 4.5 degrees. Wider tenon than Les Paul due to Explorer body geometry.

pot_shaft_holes.note: Three 3/8 inch holes drilled through body for pot shafts — 2 volume + 1 tone (Explorer uses 3-knob layout unlike Les Paul 4-knob).

toggle_switch.note: Toggle switch mounted on upper (treble) horn, separate from control cavity. Connected by wiring channel. Distinctive Explorer feature — switch is easily accessible during playing.

control_cavity.note: Rear-routed control cavity in lower body area. Explorer uses a simplified 3-knob control layout: 2 volume + 1 master tone. The toggle switch is located on the treble-side upper horn.

main_channel.note: Connects main control cavity to toggle switch on upper horn. Long channel running diagonally through the body.

pickup_channels.note: Lead wires from each pickup route to the main control cavity.

output_jack_channel.note: Short channel from control cavity to output jack on lower body edge.

output_jack.note: Standard 1/4 inch mono output jack mounted on bottom edge of body.

cover_recess.note: Shallow recess around the control cavity opening to seat the plastic cover plate flush with the body back.

hardware.tuners: Kluson Deluxe 3+3 split (original) or Grover Rotomatic (reissue)
hardware.jack: Switchcraft 1/4 inch mono, lower body edge

T2.name: 6mm Flat End Mill
T2.use: Finishing pockets (OP25–OP31), cover recess (OP35), body perimeter (OP50)

main_blank.note: Single slab body blank. All routing operations applied to this blank. Stock slightly oversized to allow fixture pins and perimeter waste.

dxf_assets.note: DXF contains a 24-vertex approximation. Should be refined with higher-resolution outline and converted to R12 (AC1009) format per project conventions.

notes: The Gibson Explorer was introduced in 1958 alongside the Flying V as part of Gibson's Modernistic series.
Only approximately 19 original Explorer guitars were shipped from the factory in 1958-59.
The original body material was korina (African limba wood), prized for its resonance and light weight.
The Explorer body is dramatically angular with asymmetric horns — the treble horn extends further than the bass horn.
The control layout differs from Les Paul: 3 knobs (2 volume, 1 master tone) instead of 4 knobs.
The toggle switch is uniquely positioned on the treble-side upper horn for easy access.
Body dimensions may vary between original '58 and reissue versions. This spec uses the original '58 dimensions.
The 24-point DXF outline is a coarse approximation — production CNC would require a higher-resolution outline with proper curves.
